pi_items.source: Task description and verifier.py line 80 check for 'tb' in program name
Task description specifies this name; verifier.py lines 86-87 check for 'enrollment' or 'alert' in notification name
DHIS2 official documentation confirms ENROLLMENT as valid program notification trigger type; verifier.py line 84 explicitly checks for trigger == 'ENROLLMENT'
Task description specifies this name; verifier.py lines 93-94 check for 'follow', 'reminder', or 'overdue' in notification name

DHIS2 documentation confirms scheduling notifications based on days before/after due dates; verifier.py line 91 checks for 'SCHEDULED' in trigger string; export_result.sh queries 'relativeScheduledDays' field which is confirmed real DHIS2 API field
Task description explicitly states '7 days after the scheduled due date'; verifier.py lines 99-101 check that days value is > 0; relativeScheduledDays is confirmed real DHIS2 API field

privileged_info_summary: The DHIS2 TB treatment notification configuration task uses the official Sierra Leone demo database (real health data, not synthetic). The task requires creating two program notifications: (1) 'TB Case Enrollment Alert' triggered on ENROLLMENT, and (2) 'TB Treatment Follow-up Reminder' triggered SCHEDULED_DAYS_DUE_DATE with 7 days delay. Both notification trigger types and configuration parameters are valid DHIS2 API fields confirmed through documentation and source code analysis. The notification names and triggers align with the verifier's validation logic that checks for specific keywords in names ('enrollment', 'alert', 'follow', 'reminder', 'overdue') and trigger types ('ENROLLMENT' and triggers containing 'SCHEDULED').
